item.title: Experience the Essence of Brazil: Where Nature, Culture, and Warmth Create Unforgettable Journeys
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Brazil is not just a destination; it’s a feeling—vibrant, soulful, and alive. It’s the pulse of a drumming circle at sunset, the silence of a jaguar sighting in the wild, the laughter shared over a seafood stew, and the awe of a waterfall thundering into the mist. Whether you’re traveling solo, with friends, or guiding families, Brazil invites you to uncover extraordinary experiences rooted in nature, culture, and deep human connection. 

From the wetlands to the coast, from wild adventures to soulful cities, here’s a glimpse of four breathtaking regions where the essence of Brazil is waiting for you. 

Mato Grosso do Sul: Nature’s Quiet Power 

Tucked in Brazil’s heartland, Mato Grosso do Sul is one of South America’s best-kept secrets. It’s a haven of wildlife, sustainability, and natural wonder. Here, you’ll find the Pantanal—the world’s largest tropical wetland, teeming with jaguars, giant anteaters, toucans, and caimans. 

Nearby, Bonito offers crystal-clear rivers and caves perfect for snorkeling, diving, and floating past schools of neon-colored fish. It’s a place where nature reveals itself gently and generously—and where conservation and tourism live side by side. For travelers seeking raw beauty with refined infrastructure, Mato Grosso do Sul is paradise on Earth. 

Learn more: https://en.visitms.com.br/  

Iguassu Falls: Nature in Full Voice 

Bordering Brazil, Argentina, and Paraguay, Iguassu Falls is one of the most powerful spectacles on the planet. With nearly 275 cascading falls framed by lush Atlantic Rainforest, this UNESCO World Heritage site stirs something ancient and awe-inspiring in all who visit. 

Walk along panoramic trails, take a boat ride into the mist, or watch the moon illuminate the Devil’s Throat on a full-moon tour. Iguassu isn’t just a sight to behold—it’s a place to feel small, connected, and alive. Paired with top-tier hotels, eco-lodges, and seamless transportation, it offers a rare combination of wild energy and guest comfort. 

Learn more: https://www.iguassu.com.br/para-visitantes-destino-iguacu/  

Ceará: Wind, Waves & Coastal Joy 

In Brazil’s northeast, Ceará dances with sunlight and sea breezes. Known for its dramatic dunes, turquoise lagoons, and endless coastline, this region is a playground for adventure seekers and dreamers alike. 

Jericoacoara, once a hidden fishing village, now charms visitors with its barefoot luxury, kite-surfing vibes, and breathtaking sunsets from the famous dune. Inland, you’ll find rich cultural traditions, vibrant crafts, and music that drifts through cobblestone streets. Ceará is Brazil at its most laid-back and luminous—a place where time slows, and life shines. 

Learn more: https://visiteceara.com/  

Bahia: Where Culture Sings 

In Bahia, Brazil’s African heritage comes to life in a powerful blend of music, spirituality, food, and tradition. Walk the historic streets of Salvador, where colonial churches stand beside colorful facades, and capoeira rhythms echo through open plazas. 

The beaches of Bahia are as soulful as its cities—offering places to relax, surf, sail, and celebrate. Taste spicy moqueca, dance to the beat of axé, or join in the timeless energy of a festival. Bahia’s hospitality is more than a welcome—it’s an embrace. 

Learn more: https://www.bahia.com.br  

For Every Type of Traveler - Essence of Brazil

  • Solo travelers will find both solitude and connection—through nature, culture, and community. 
  • Friends and couples can chase adventure, discover hidden beaches, and dance until sunrise. 
  • Families will enjoy safe, fun, and enriching experiences, from snorkeling in Bonito to boat rides at Iguassu and crafts markets in Bahia. 

No matter the passion—wildlife, luxury, wellness, culture, or cuisine—Brazil offers not just variety, but soul. 

About the “Brasil DNA” Campaign 

This content is part of the Brasil DNA campaign, a strategic initiative created by GVA (Global Vision Access) in partnership with Embratur, to spotlight the core identity of Brazil’s tourism experiences. The campaign brings attention to what truly defines the country: its natural treasures, warmth of its people, and the exceptional infrastructure that makes it all accessible. Through storytelling, training, and inspiration, Brasil DNA invites the world to reconnect with Brazil’s most authentic self.

Learn more: www.brasildna.com

item.title: A Journey Through the Flavors of Brazil
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Flavors of Brazil? Brazil captivates travelers from all over the world with its natural beauty, but what truly lingers in memory is its cuisine. From north to south, east to west, diverse cultural influences have created surprising and unforgettable flavors. Come along with us to discover some of the country’s delights that go far beyond the famous feijoada and caipirinha.

Flavors of Brazil - Baião de Dois

More than just a perfect destination for sun, sea, and kitesurfing lovers, Ceará also offers unmistakable culinary treasures. Baião de dois is a hearty, nutritious dish made with a mix of rice, cowpea beans, and curd cheese. It was originally created to meet the needs of workers in the Ceará hinterlands.

Flavors of Brazil - Caldo de Piranha


You’ve probably heard of piranhas—those carnivorous fish known for their sharp teeth, abundant in the Pantanal wetlands of Mato Grosso do Sul. One of the most memorable experiences in this region is fishing for these creatures and having them cooked by the chefs on your boat or lodge into a delicious stew with fresh vegetables and herbs.

Flavors of Brazil -Acarajé

Beyond its coconut-fringed beaches, Bahia dazzles visitors with its explosion of flavors. One of the state’s most traditional and unmissable dishes is acarajé, a symbol of Afro-Brazilian culture. It’s a fritter made from seasoned black-eyed pea dough, fried in dendê (palm oil), and stuffed with vatapá (a rich paste of coconut milk, cashew nuts, peanuts, and shrimp), vinaigrette, and dried shrimp. To finish, chili pepper is added to taste—though traditionally, it’s served quite spicy.

Pirá de Foz

Home to one of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World, Foz do Iguaçu also stands out for its freshwater fish in the dish known as pirá de foz. Created in this city in Paraná state, it features surubim fish, typical of the region, cooked in a ginger sauce and served with manioc purée and spinach rice.

Unlike acarajé, which has been enjoyed in Bahia for centuries, pirá de foz was recognized as an iconic local dish through a gastronomic contest in 1996.

Craving a taste already? Blumar, a specialized tour operator, can help you plan your culinary journey across Brazil, creating fascinating and unforgettable experiences.

Want to discover more of Brazil’s treasures? Visit the Brasil DNA website.

.

Por Elaine Villatoro, do Live More, Travel More

item.title: Travel to Brazil: A Journey Outward and Inward
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Ah, Brazil! In this vast South American country, nature displays itself in countless shades, sounds, textures, aromas, and flavors, while its people welcome visitors with open arms, a smile on their face, and a freshly brewed coffee. Traveling to new places and cultures has the power to shape and reshape us as human beings, and Brazil is without a doubt a transformative destination for those who visit.

If your client needs a break to rethink their way of life, reconnect with nature, and reconnect with themselves, this destination will surprise them — and Blumar is the perfect DMC to help create the perfect itinerary. Below are some truly special experiences:

Disconnecting on Paradise Beaches

There are many beautiful beaches around the world, but few remain truly paradisiacal, with little human interference. Destinations such as Boipeba Island and the Maraú Peninsula, located in the southern part of the state of Bahia, are some of these hidden treasures.

For those who define luxury as tranquility and limited access, Boipeba is the ideal choice, as cars are not even allowed on the island. Trails through mangroves, bioluminescence observation, and locals who fight to this day to preserve the area will make the trip unforgettable. Whether with a stunning view from the top of a hill at Pousada Mangabeiras or with your feet in the sand at Ventos Moreré, the sound of birds and massage services guarantee moments of relaxation.

With a bit more infrastructure — since it is possible to get around by car (although a 4x4 is recommended due to the dirt roads) — the Maraú Peninsula enchants everyone with its natural pools, giant bromeliads, and the beautiful Cassange Lagoon, where you can go kayaking and stand-up paddling. One of the most remarkable accommodations here is Casa dos Arandis, which not only supports sustainable projects but also offers guests many ways to connect with nature, their bodies, and their own essence through sunrise yoga classes and meals prepared with organic ingredients.

Travel to Brazil: Learning From Wildlife

Surrounded by technology and daily obligations, we often forget that we are also part of nature. Ethical observation of wild animals is a practice that not only helps protect them but also reminds us that we are part of an ecosystem and that our daily choices impact the whole. Blumar can help coordinate visits to parks and serious conservation projects across Brazil.

Those planning to visit the state of Rio de Janeiro can explore the Golden Lion Tamarin Ecological Park, where it is possible to feel the magic of the Atlantic Forest up close and, with some luck, spot these endangered primates during hikes on foot and in 4x4 vehicles.

In the heart of the Cerrado — Brazil’s second-largest biome and the most biodiverse savanna in the world — lies the sophisticated Pousada Trijunção. More than just accommodation, Trijunção contributes to the formation of an ecological corridor with Grande Sertão Veredas National Park. In partnership with the Onçafari Institute, the lodge promotes sightings of the maned wolf, the largest wild canid in South America.

These are just a few suggestions, but the options are endless! Blumar is a DMC with 40 years of experience that can help you organize a journey throughout the entire country — from logistics to activities that will create unforgettable memories.

Would you like to discover more about Brazil’s wonders? Visit the Brazil DNA website: https://bureaumundo.com/brasil-dna/

.

Written by: Elaine Villatoro - Live More, Travel More

item.title: Natural wonders of Brazil: National treasures that amaze visitors
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Discover the natural wonders of Brazil - A country with such a rich and extensive ethnic and cultural diversity as Brazil could not fail to enchant with its breathtaking scenery and landscapes. From parks to paradise-like beaches, Brazilian diversity and charm extend to its national treasures.

Chapada da Diamantina – Bahia

Known for its breathtaking views, Chapada da Diamantina is a perfect destination for ecotourism enthusiasts and practitioners, but also for those who wish to have a deeper connection with the environment.

The region is well known for its imposing waterfalls that stand out amid the lush greenery and for its cascades that make the place even more special. Although there are more than 360 waterfalls, the most outstanding ones are the Cachoeira da Fumaça, considered one of the largest in Brazil, Cachoeira do Buracão, famous for its impressive scenery, and Cachoeira do Mosquito, with its beautiful water flows descending between towering rock walls. The region also has the famous pools formed inside caves, which, when illuminated by a beam of light, create a magical and unique sight. The highlights among them are Poço Azul, Poço Encantado, and Gruta da Pratinha.

The charms of Chapada da Diamantina attract many nature lovers with its landscapes that move any visitor, but also catch the attention of adventure sports practitioners, with activities ranging from trails to rappelling and climbing, showing that the region pleases all kinds of preferences.

Barra dos Remédios Beach – Ceará

Often called the “Caribbean of Ceará,” Barra dos Remédios Beach has been drawing attention for its enchanting turquoise-blue sea, crystal-clear waters, and the formation of natural pools during low tide. Alongside the sand dunes blending into the sea, creating an exotic and paradisiacal landscape, the beach—still relatively unknown—offers visitors a tranquil and authentic experience.

Beyond the unforgettable visual experience, the location offers other activities and attractions, such as buggy rides that explore the dunes and help you get to know the region better, the meeting of the Remédios River with the sea (which creates natural pools and a perfect setting for a freshwater swim), and the spectacular sunset over the dunes, providing an incredible panoramic view.

Barra dos Remédios Beach remains etched in the memory of those who visit it, offering the tranquility and comfort so many seek amid a chaotic and stressful routine.

Buraco das Araras – Mato Grosso do Sul

Have you ever imagined a massive sinkhole over 100 meters deep serving as home to 120 red macaws and more than 150 other bird species? This setting exists and is one of the most remarkable tourist attractions, bringing visitors closer to the fauna of Mato Grosso do Sul.

The sinkhole, a cavity formed by rock decomposition over time—in this case, a process that took 300,000 years—is known as Buraco das Araras. It attracts many visitors and students from various fields thanks to its rich biodiversity and the privileged view of hundreds of red macaws coloring the sky with their presence. In addition to the red macaws, there are over 150 bird species, alligators, small rodents, monkeys, bats, and snakes. A true paradise for those who love to admire local wildlife and study native species.

Access to the site is easy, with a 970-meter trail, and visitors are astonished by the unique beauty of the macaws and the other animals inhabiting the sinkhole. The site also makes a significant contribution to ecotourism, which is carefully controlled to avoid negative environmental impacts and to raise awareness among tourists about the importance of conservation.

Iguassu Falls – Foz do Iguaçu

One of Brazil’s most iconic landmarks, Iguaçu Falls captivates everyone with its displays of water power, with the main and largest attraction being the Devil’s Throat, a monumental drop that plunges vertically, creating a mist that envelops the landscape in a dazzling spectacle.

Visitors enjoy an immersive experience through various tours, such as the Trilha das Cataratas (Falls Trail), which offers a panoramic view; the Panoramic Elevator, which provides a privileged lookout point; Macuco Safari, a boat ride that takes you to the base of the falls; Porto Canoas, a food court with direct views of the Iguaçu River; and the Helisul Experience, a helicopter ride over the falls.

Beyond its magnificent waterfalls, the surrounding region preserves a subtropical forest that is home to a wide variety of endangered species, such as the jaguar, tapir, giant anteater, and purple-breasted parrot, making it the ideal destination for those seeking closer contact with nature. In addition to protecting local flora and fauna, the Falls also shelter several archaeological sites and rock paintings left by indigenous peoples who inhabited the region thousands of years ago, ensuring the preservation and respect of their history.

Much more than just landscapes, the national treasures found only here tell an important and meaningful part of a people’s story and show that Brazil’s richness can be discovered in every corner.

These are just a few suggestions, but the possibilities are endless! Blumar, a Destination Management Company (DMC) with over 40 years of expertise, creates tailor-made travel experiences across Brazil — from logistics to unique activities that turn into unforgettable memories. If you’d like to explore these experiences, simply ask your preferred travel agency to get in touch with Blumar, as they offer a wide range of ready-to-book programs designed to help you experience Brazil at its very best.

Discover more about Brasil DNA: https://bureaumundo.com/brasil-dna/

.

Written by: Ana Carolina Lack de Souza

item.title: Brazilian Regional Festivals: What truly represents Brazil’s historical richness
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Do you know any of the Brazilian regional festivals? Brazil is known for its authenticity, hospitality, and natural beauty, but the country goes far beyond that. What truly represents Brazil’s historical richness and cultural diversity are the regional festivals held in every part of the country, each one telling its own story, with traditions, music, and elements unique to different states.

Fartal – Foz do Iguaçu


One of the most famous events in the region, Fartal brings together all of Foz do Iguaçu’s cultural diversity in one place. The fair takes place on the city’s anniversary and features stands ranging from local cuisine to handicrafts made by local artists. Although admission is free, participants are encouraged to donate coats, blankets, and clothes to the “Warm Up Foz” campaign, as well as non-perishable food, which is directed to the projects Núcleo Criança de Valor and Um Chute para o Futuro.

Beyond showcasing Foz’s cultural wealth and diversity, Fartal also promotes donation campaigns that benefit both children and people in vulnerable social situations.

Iemanjá Festival – Bahia


Recognized in 2022 as part of Salvador’s cultural heritage, this is one of the city’s most important religious and cultural manifestations. The tradition began with fishermen from the Rio Vermelho neighborhood, who offered gifts to the deities of the sea, hoping for protection and an end to fish shortages that threatened their livelihoods.

Celebrated on February 2nd, the festival begins in Rio Vermelho and culminates with boat departures, when people offer flowers and gifts to the orixá as acts of gratitude and devotion. The celebration also features samba groups, percussion, capoeira, and large musical performances.

Festa Junina – Ceará


Known as one of the most vibrant and traditional festivals in Ceará, this celebration reflects the cultural and historical richness of the region. Originally brought by the Portuguese, over the years it incorporated local traditions, foods, and customs. The São João festivities in Ceará stand out for their quadrilha dance competitions, with colorful, detailed costumes and lively performances. Music such as forró, xote, and baião set the rhythm for nights of celebration that last the entire month, along with traditional foods like pamonha (corn cake), corn on the cob, rapadura (sugarcane sweet), and canjica (corn pudding), which are loved by many.

Rodeos – Mato Grosso do Sul


In Mato Grosso do Sul, rodeos go far beyond bull riding and competitions. They represent the identity of the people, who preserve the values and traditions of rural life. Over time, the event grew from being just a competition among cowboys into a large-scale spectacle that combines sports, music, dances, and live performances. Rooted in the state’s strong cattle ranching traditions and influenced by Mexican and Spanish customs that arrived in Brazil in the 1950s, rodeos are a profound expression of the sertanejo culture of Mato Grosso do Sul. More than just a festival, they celebrate the lifestyle and traditions of rural communities.

These Brazilian regional festivals show that the country’s richness and diversity go far beyond landscapes and beaches. They reveal that Brazil’s true beauty is found in its faith, music, food, and the joy of celebrating each unique story and tradition.

Discover more about Brasil DNA: https://bureaumundo.com/brasil-dna/

.

Written by: Ana Carolina Lack de Souza

item.title: Travel Through Taste: Discover Brazil’s diverse gastronomy
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Brazil is a country where flavors mirror its vast landscapes, cultural heritage, and history. Each region tells its story through food: indigenous roots, African traditions, Portuguese influences, and waves of migration have all shaped how Brazilians cook, celebrate, and share meals.

While yuca, beans, rice, and tropical fruits form the national base, the taste of Brazil changes as you move across the map. In the Northeast, spices, seafood, and dendê oil define bold dishes. In the South and interior, cattle ranching, freshwater fish, and hearty stews take center stage. Along rivers and wetlands, recipes connect directly to the cycles of water and land.

For travelers, Brazilian gastronomy is not just about eating—it is about experiencing history, community, and landscapes through food.

Spotlight on Regional Flavors and Experiences

Bahia – Cuisine of Memory and Resistance
Bahia’s gastronomy is one of Brazil’s most iconic, rooted in Afro-Brazilian heritage. Dishes like acarajé, moqueca, and vatapá highlight bold ingredients such as palm oil, coconut milk, peppers, and fresh seafood. More than flavors, Bahian food carries memory and resilience: every plate reflects centuries of cultural blending.

Imagine walking through Salvador’s Pelourinho, the scent of dendê oil in the air as a baiana hands you a steaming acarajé. Join a cooking class to prepare a traditional moqueca with fish caught that very morning, and discover how food connects with Bahia’s cultural and spiritual traditions.

Ceará – The Beauty on the Table

In Ceará, indigenous staples like cassava meet Portuguese sweets and African influences. Signature dishes include peixada cearense, sarapatel, and munguzá. The state’s cuisine is festive and vibrant, especially during Festas Juninas.

Explore the Ceará Gastronomic Route, where Fortaleza’s lively markets and fishing villages serve fresh seafood and tapioca-based snacks. In June, immerse yourself in São João celebrations, tasting corn cakes, roasted peanuts, and coconut sweets under colorful lanterns and forró music.

Mato Grosso do Sul – Pantanal Gastronomy


The Pantanal, the world’s largest wetland, offers a table shaped by rivers and cattle ranching. Travelers taste moqueca de pintado, coxinha de jacaré, quibe de barbado, and even sashimi de piranha.

Discover the Pantanal Gastronomic Route, which links inns, riverside restaurants, and local kitchens. Picture yourself grilling freshly caught fish over an open fire by the river, or joining a Pantaneiro barbecue under a star-filled sky, where food is inseparable from the landscape and way of life.

Iguassu Falls – A Borderland of Flavors


At the meeting point of Brazil, Argentina, and Paraguay, Iguassu Falls blends cultures and cuisines. Guarani traditions, river fish, and tropical fruits combine with immigrant influences and Brazilian churrasco.

After marveling at the Iguaçu Falls, take a border gastronomy tour, tasting Argentinian empanadas, Paraguayan chipa, and Brazilian barbecue—all in one evening. Local festivals highlight the city’s multicultural character, where every meal is part of the tri-border story.

An Invitation to Taste Brazil

For North American travelers, discovering Brazil means tasting the stories of its people. From street markets in Salvador, to seafood villages in Ceará, to riverside dinners in the Pantanal, and borderland blends in Foz do Iguaçu—every meal is an immersion into culture, tradition, and community.

Craving a taste already? Blumar, a specialized tour operator, can help you plan your culinary journey across Brazil, creating fascinating and unforgettable experiences.

Plan your culinary journey to Brazil today—and discover flavors you will never forget: https://bureaumundo.com/brasil-dna/

item.title: From Ceará to the Pantanal: Brazil establishes itself as a wellness tourism destination
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Taking care of physical and mental health is no longer just a detail of a trip — it has become a priority. Wellness tourism is already the fourth-largest sector of the vast global health economy, behind personal care, nutrition, and physical activity, according to the Global Wellness Economy Monitor 2024 by the Global Wellness Institute (GWI).

In this context — which includes not only spas but also nature-based itineraries, digital detox programs, farm-to-table culinary experiences, and sleep retreats — Brazil stands out by offering experiences that combine the country’s privileged natural settings with culture and high-quality services in the sector.

Brazil ranks as the 12th largest wellness market in the world and holds first place in the Latin America and Caribbean region among the 46 countries evaluated by the GWI, showing that wellness tourists in Brazil find an already well-established sector.

Brazil’s nature as a wellness backdrop

From north to south, the country holds destinations where nature and wellness meet, forming a perfect combination. In Ceará, the constant wind and the sea create the ideal setting for renewal: beach meditation, sunrise and sunset outings, and trending sports like kitesurfing and windsurfing free the mind from worries. The Carmel Charme Resort, in Aquiraz, for instance, features a well-equipped spa, but what truly enhances the body-and-soul-healing experiences are activities such as yoga classes with ocean views, the Hands in Clay workshop, and seaside picnics with cushions and blankets laid out on the lawn.

Still in the Northeast, Bahia’s natural landscapes — unspoiled beaches and endless coconut groves — are enough on their own to inspire relaxation. A good example is Boipeba Island, which has been attracting travelers seeking connection (and reconnection) with the sea, the sand, and the lush greenery of the region. For those who prefer high-end wellness care, Trancoso is the place to go, home to resorts and boutique hotels with luxury spas, such as UXUA, Fasano, Club Med, and Pousada Estrela d’Água.

While the Northeast beaches provide settings for massages, sports, and contemplation, in Foz do Iguaçu it is the force of the waterfalls and the forest that define wellness activities. Visitors can experience forest bathing, or shinrin-yoku, by walking along the Indian Trail within the Atlantic Forest, engaging in tree-touching, bioenergetic exercises, and contact with Guarani medicinal plants. For those seeking mental balance, outdoor yoga and mindfulness classes provide breathing techniques and stretches that help reset the mind, oxygenate the body, and anchor oneself in the present moment. Moreover, the natural energy of the Iguaçu Falls — one of the most energetic places on the planet — can be felt through ion therapy: along the Falls Trail, stops are made for exercises and to absorb the negative ions produced by the mist of the waterfalls.

Another example of a perfect Brazilian wellness destination is Mato Grosso do Sul. In Bonito, crystal-clear waters and lush greenery become spaces for open-air yoga and relaxing massages, in addition to waterfalls, caves, and trails that help even the busiest travelers slow down. Many resorts and eco-lodges offer early morning yoga classes overlooking rivers and waterfalls, fostering complete relaxation. In the Pantanal, no great effort is needed to fully connect with nature. In one of the largest wildlife sanctuaries in the world, a large spa infrastructure, massages, or pools are hardly necessary.

In this region, where human impact is minimal and nature sets the rules, immersive experiences abound in inns and eco-ranches. Guided hikes, safaris, horseback rides, and boat tours — like those offered at Caiman and Fazenda San Francisco — make visitors forget all about city stress.

For tourism professionals looking to transform Brazil’s wellness potential into authentic itineraries, Blumar Turismo — one of Brazil’s most experienced and renowned tour operators — offers expertise as a DMC, supporting international travel agents and operators in translating Brazil’s diversity into memorable experiences.

Want to learn more about Brazil? Visit the Brasil DNA website.

.

By: Natália Manczyk, from Porta de Embarque

item.title: Brazil’s Diversity – Nurtured by History and Resilience
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
When people think of Brazil, the first images that often come to mind are the familiar stereotypes of samba, carnival, and soccer. While these elements are indeed part of Brazilian culture, our country is far more diverse — shaped by unique histories, identities, and traditions. Each state carries its own story and cultural essence, making every region distinct and incomparable.

Across Brazil, visitors are often astonished and enchanted by the immense variety of cultures, traditions, and customs that coexist, revealing the beauty and individuality of every corner of the country.

Bahia

Bahia is known as the land of Brazilian ancestry — a state filled with history and rich culture that can be seen, felt, and experienced in every corner. What sets Bahia apart is the profound African influence visible in its traditions, music, religion, and annual festivals. Still, the Bahian identity is a complex blend of many peoples, reflected in its traditions and unique forms of expression.

The story of Bahian culture began with the first encounters between the Indigenous peoples who inhabited the region and the Portuguese colonizers. The customs and traditions of both groups laid the foundations of what would later evolve into Bahian culture. With the arrival of Africans brought to Bahia, their habits and values merged with the existing traditions, creating a rich fusion of three cultural roots — Indigenous, African, and European — that gave birth to Bahia’s multicultural identity.

This blend can be most vividly appreciated in Bahian cuisine — from acarajé to moqueca and vatapá — as well as in festivals like the Festa de Iemanjá and Festa do Senhor do Bonfim. Traditional dances, such as capoeira and the samba circles of the Recôncavo region, captivate audiences and tell the story of Bahia’s deep ancestral roots.

Mato Grosso do Sul

Beyond its iconic Pantanal wetlands, the culture and history of Mato Grosso do Sul reflect the vast diversity of the peoples who have shaped its identity. The charm of the state extends beyond its landscapes — it lies in its multicultural essence.

Rooted in the early interactions between Portuguese settlers and Indigenous groups during colonization, the state’s culture was later enriched by immigrants from various countries and regions. This mix of influences has been preserved and celebrated by the people of Mato Grosso do Sul.

The harmonious blend of Gaucho, Paraguayan, Bolivian, Japanese, and Indigenous traditions — seemingly different yet deeply connected — has created a unique cultural identity found only in this region. This diversity can be experienced through its rodeos, celebrated in major local events, its cuisine, and its living customs.

Ceará

The culture of Ceará is defined by a history of struggle and resilience — traits that have shaped its people’s way of life and identity. Ceará’s story is deeply marked by endurance, but also by creativity and diversity, born from centuries of cultural exchange.

From the first encounters between the Indigenous peoples and the Portuguese to the later arrival of Africans, these early interactions laid the foundation for Ceará’s unique cultural blend.

This convergence of such distinct influences can be seen in the state’s music, dance, cuisine, museums, festivals, and artistic expressions — all of which keep Ceará’s history alive and celebrated.

Iguassu Falls

Iguassu Falls is a sacred land — a territory pulsating with ancestry and multiculturalism. Long before the Portuguese and Spanish arrived, it was home to Indigenous peoples whose legacy remains alive in the region.

These early encounters were the first of many cultural exchanges that shaped Foz into what it is today. Over time, waves of immigrants — Paraguayans, Argentinians, Arabs, Chinese, and others — added new layers to its identity. The result is a city enriched by contrasts and connections between diverse cultures.

The fusion of such different traditions has shaped Iguassu’s identity, which remains vibrant and significant to this day. Beyond its natural wonder — the majestic Iguaçu Falls — stands out for its diverse character, artistic expressions, and the living heritage of its peoples that continue to honor their ancestral roots.

For travel professionals seeking to transform Brazil’s potential for well-being into authentic itineraries, Blumar Turismo — one of Brazil’s most experienced and respected destination management companies (DMCs) — offers its expertise to help international travel agents and tour operators translate Brazil’s cultural diversity into unforgettable travel experiences.

More than the stereotypes often associated with it, Brazil reveals that each state is unique, diverse, and defined by its own stories and characteristics — proof that our richness lies in the details, and that diversity runs deep in every aspect of who we are.

.

Text written by: Ana Carolina Lack

item.title: The Charms of Boipeba Island
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Bahia is known for its cultural richness, paradisiacal landscapes, and its large historical heritage. However, the main destinations are the beautiful islands and beaches that take anyone’s breath away. One of the highlights is Boipeba Island, considered one of the most beautiful in Brazil. 

With calm and isolated beaches, Boipeba Island, although not a very well-known destination, has started to draw attention for its paradisiacal landscapes and for being a perfect place for those who want to forget the stress and chaos of routine. The archipelago, besides being considered one of the most enchanting places in Bahia, is located between the Rio do Inferno, Tinharé Island, and the municipality of Cairu.

And they are the only inhabited archipelagos that maintain economic activities. One of the factors that attract visitors and create a sense of “isolation” is te fact that cars are not allowed on the island, the main forms of transportation are quad bikes ant tractors.

How to Get to the Island 

To reach the island and enjoy its various attractions, the most common way is to take a ferry from Salvador to Bom Despacho or Itaparica, followed by a bus or van to Valença, and finally a speedboat from Valença to Boipeba. There is also a faster way to get to the island, which is taking an air taxi from Salvador to a point near Boipeba.

Main Attractions of Boipeba 

In addition to enjoying several beaches that allow close contact with nature, such as Cueira, Tassimirim, Bainema, and Moreré, the island offers other types of tours, including private boat trips that take visitors to various beaches and natural pools, such as those of Bainema, Castelhanis, among others.

There is also an incredible immersion and contact with nature through kayaking visits to the white mangroves and observing the bioluminescence phenomenon of plankton; and for animal lovers, it is possible to take a boat trip to see humpback whales.

Cuisine and Inns 

Competing with the charm of the place, the inns of Boipeba are not lacking in comfort, beauty, and a wide variety of styles. Ranging from rustic inns, such as Eco-Pousada Casa Bobô, to more sophisticated ones, such as Pousada Coco Bambu Moreré.

The region of Moreré is one of the most visited on the island, thanks to its beautiful natural landscapes and the various restaurants that showcase the region’s extensive gastronomic richness.

The most famous ones, both for their food and their privileged views, are: 

  • Brisa e Mar Restaurant; 
  • Mar e Coco Restaurant; 
  • Casa Filtro Restaurant; 
  • Paraíso Moreré Restaurant. 

Climate 

Planning and checking the island’s weather during the travel period is extremely important to make the most of what the island has to offer. 

From December to March: the best moment for those who want to enjoy high season, sunny days, and calm seas. 

From September to November: great for those who like pleasant weather with little rain, fewer tourists, a more affordable prices in inns and hotels.

From July to November: for those who love humpback whale watching, this is the best time to take tours and see them. 

Diversity and Preservation of Local History 

The villages of Boipeba offer diverse and rich culture, with fascinating 17th-century architecture, preservation of religiosity, gastronomy, and ancestral practices that remain a unique characteristic and ensure the beauty of the place.

Being surrounded by the Atlantic Ocean, it has a wide variety of fish and bird species, as well as rich terrestrial fauna that includes foxes, armadillos, otters, and monkeys.

Boipeba Island deeply connects and captivates those who visit it with its almost untouched nature, its culture, and gastronomy preserved by the locals, surprising visitors with its climate, fauna, and paradisiacal landscapes.

Text writen by: Ana Carolina Lack

item.title: Brazil's Vibrant Holiday Season: Authentic Christmas Spirit and Unique Destination Experiences
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
For travel professionals seeking unique seasonal offerings, Brazil presents an enticing opportunity to enrich holiday itineraries with destinations that pair authentic local culture and celebration with world-class natural and sustainable experiences. From the heart of the countryside in Mato Grosso do Sul to the breathtaking landscapes of Iguassu Falls, the festive season becomes a gateway to immerse travelers in both tradition and adventure.

These destinations not only offer festive activities during Christmas, but also highlight Brazil's commitment to sustainability, outdoor exploration and cultural richness. Whether clients are looking for family-friendly Christmas programs, eco-conscious travel combinations or dynamic cultural engagement, these experiences showcase why Brazil continues to captivate global visitors.

Mato Grosso do Sul - Authentic Countryside Christmas Experiences

Mato Grosso do Sul, known globally for its spectacular Pantanal wetlands and Bonito's renowned ecotourism attractions, also brings to life one of Brazil's most heartfelt Christmas seasons. Throughout December, towns and cities across the state come alive with themed decorations, community concerts, outdoor gatherings and creative installations that embrace the holiday spirit under warm tropical skies.

In Caracol, the beloved tradition of Santa Claus arrives each year with delightful surprises, from appearances on horseback and in giant gift boxes to imaginative descents from themed floats that captivate locals and visitors alike. The event opens a vibrant holiday program featuring lights, cultural performances and outdoor festivities in Praça 10 de Maio.

Três Lagoas invites visitors to its Natal Encantado parade, where Santa Claus leads a joyful procession from the Fire Department through Lagoa Maior toward light-filled celebrations complete with music, dance and even freestyle rap battles. Meanwhile, Bonito seamlessly blends its natural ecotourism charm with festival spectacles such as the Natal Mais Bonito event, featuring ballet, choirs and flash mobs amidst beautifully illuminated squares.

In Dourados, families and travelers are welcomed by an impressive LED Christmas tree, light tunnels and community celebrations, while Campo Grande, the state capital, offers an extensive 33-day program of concerts, theater and festive parades that make the season truly memorable.

Iguassu Falls - A Unique Tropical Christmas Destination

At Iguassu Falls, holiday celebrations are as breathtaking as the destination's iconic natural wonder itself. Recognized globally as one of the New 7 Natural Wonders of the World, Iguassu Falls becomes a festive backdrop where nature and holiday spirit harmonize in unforgettable ways. From late November through December, the destination hosts a special Christmas program that blends themed decorations, musical performances and family-friendly festivities within the stunning landscapes of the national park.

One highlight of the season is "O Canto das Águas", featuring the Paraguayan orchestra H2O Sonidos de la Tierra, the world's first ensemble to transform water into music - a truly unique cultural experience set alongside the world-famous waterfalls. Beyond that, Gramadão da Vila A lights up with festive installations, live shows, theater performances and arts workshops that run into early January.

Local hotels and resorts in Iguassu Falls elevate the season with special holiday packages, family activities, themed entertainment and festive dining, ensuring visitors enjoy both comfort and celebration throughout their stay. The combination of warm weather, cultural events and remarkable natural settings makes Iguassu Falls an exceptional choice for travelers seeking an inspiring holiday destination.

About Brazil as a Holiday Destination

As Brazil continues to attract global travelers with its rich cultural heritage and remarkable natural landscapes, the country is experiencing an unprecedented moment in international tourism. In 2025, Brazil celebrated a historic milestone by welcoming more than 8 million international visitors, a record number that reflects the nation's growing appeal in the global travel market and positions it for an estimated over 9 million arrivals by year-end.

From vibrant Christmas celebrations in the heart of the countryside to awe-inspiring natural wonders like Iguassu Falls, Brazil offers endless possibilities for holiday experiences that blend festive cheer with sustainability, culture and adventure. These compelling seasonal offerings provide travel professionals with engaging, marketable experiences that resonate with culturally curious, adventure-seeking and sustainability-minded audiences worldwide.

item.title: New Year’s Eve in Bonito – The blend of New with nature taking center stage Year celebrations
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
When we talk about New Year’s Eve, culturally, it is very common to celebrate this festive date on beaches, but Bonito emerges as a perfect destination for those who enjoy celebrating in a quieter place surrounded by nature.

Well known for its beautiful landscapes and diverse natural attractions, Bonito has been establishing itself as a destination sought after by those who wish to celebrate the New Year in a different way.

Many aspects make Bonito an ideal region to enjoy New Year’s Eve, and one of them is the perfect climate for outdoor activities.

With warm temperatures and bright sunshine, it is the perfect opportunity to spend the day exploring caves, waterfalls, and crystal-clear rivers.

And there are several ways to celebrate the arrival of a new year, whether amid the tranquility of a riverside area or at a lodge surrounded by nature.

New Year’s Eve at Praça das Piraputangas

Praça das Piraputangas is the heart of public celebrations in Bonito. This space brings together families, friends, and tourists to celebrate the arrival of the New Year in a simple and joyful way, yet full of meaning.

With live music performances and various food and drink options, in a cheerful and welcoming atmosphere.

Réveillon Bonito de La Musique

One of the city’s best-known events, this party combines sophistication, good music, and plenty of fun in a unique setting. With famous DJs, live shows, and impeccable production, it is ideal for those who love a lively and stylish celebration.

The event takes place in a specially prepared venue, with themed décor and high-quality services such as open bar and open food. It is the right choice for those who want an unforgettable New Year’s Eve in a stunning setting.

New Year’s Eve at Zagaia

Hotel Zagaia Eco Resort, a resort that is part of the city’s hotel offerings, also prepares a special celebration for New Year’s Eve. With a sophisticated dinner, live music, and an incredible view of the resort’s green areas, New Year’s Eve at Zagaia is perfect for those seeking comfort and exclusivity.

In addition, the resort is known for its impeccable infrastructure and quality service, ensuring an unforgettable experience for guests and partygoers.

Bonito Boutique New Year’s Eve

Bonito Boutique New Year’s Eve is known for its elegant and intimate atmosphere, ideal for those who prefer a more reserved and exclusive event.

With carefully crafted menus, live music, and a thoughtfully decorated setting, this option is perfect for those who value personalized, high-end experiences.

With a diverse program and options that cater to different celebration styles, Bonito presents itself as a destination to be considered when planning New Year’s Eve. The range of events, combined with the city’s natural attractions and tourism infrastructure, allows each visitor to create their own experience for welcoming the new year.

item.title: Why Is Brazil Special? Warmth, Diversity and Tourism Growth Win Over the World
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Brazil is experiencing a historic moment in international tourism. According to a statement from Embratur and the Ministry of Tourism, the country reached an unprecedented milestone of 8 million international tourists in 2025, confirming Brazil’s growing prominence on the global travel map.

One of the symbols of this expansion is the launch of the “turistômetros”, digital panels installed in Rio de Janeiro and Brasília that display, in real time, the number of international arrivals. The initiative highlights the steady growth of inbound tourism and reinforces transparency in monitoring the sector’s performance.

For Marcelo Freixo, president of Embratur, the numbers go far beyond statistics. “It is a spectacular number. Brazil, which never reached 7 million, is now reaching 9 million in one year,” he said. According to Freixo, the most significant impact lies in job creation and income generation, positioning tourism as a strategic driver of Brazil’s socioeconomic development.

This new phase of growth is also reflected on social media. Videos, testimonials and travel content shared by international visitors reveal that, beyond Brazil’s famous beaches and exuberant natural landscapes, Brazilian hospitality stands out as one of the country’s greatest assets. For many travelers and influencers, the sense of warmth and human connection is what makes Brazil truly unforgettable.

American influencer Tim Cunningham, from the channel TimExplica, describes this appeal through simple everyday experiences. “In Brazil, I love going into a bakery in the morning, having a coffee and eating a misto quente while chatting with the waiter,” he says. “I really enjoy hitting the road in Brazil and traveling around. It is a country with many options for tourists.”

The country’s diverse geography further strengthens its appeal. Paradisiacal beaches, tropical forests, historic towns and vibrant metropolises coexist within short distances, allowing travelers to enjoy multiple experiences in a single trip. Brazilian gastronomy also plays a central role, combining Indigenous, African and European influences that consistently impress international visitors.

French traveler Lorea Bergerot, who moved to Brazil in 2024, says her adaptation process was marked by pleasant surprises. “I fell in love with the landscapes in Brazil and with the number of places you can reach within a few hours by car,” she explains. “Not to mention that it’s warm almost all year round.”

With rising visitor numbers, strong cultural identity and a welcoming spirit recognized worldwide, Brazil continues to strengthen its position as a destination that offers far more than natural beauty, delivering memorable experiences shaped by people, diversity and connection.

item.title: End-of-year festivities in Bahia: What to do in Brazil’s most vibrant summer destination
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Bahia becomes one of Brazil’s most exciting destinations during the end-of-year festivities. With the arrival of summer, the state brings together music, culture, natural landscapes and an intense program that attracts visitors from all over the country and around the world.

In Salvador, the highlight is the traditional Virada Salvador Festival, one of the largest New Year’s Eve celebrations in Brazil, featuring major national music acts in free beachfront concerts. The capital also offers private parties, special hotel dinners, fireworks displays and celebrations that blend rhythms such as axé, samba, pop and electronic music. During the day, beaches like Porto da Barra, Farol da Barra and Flamengo become gathering spots for those seeking sun, sea and a festive atmosphere.

Along the northern coast, destinations such as Praia do Forte, Guarajuba and Imbassaí attract visitors looking for a more sophisticated New Year’s Eve, with exclusive parties, beach clubs, resorts with special programs and experiences that combine comfort, nature and high-end gastronomy.

In southern Bahia, Trancoso and Arraial d’Ajuda stand out as some of the most sought-after New Year’s destinations. The villages feature special decorations, Trancoso’s Quadrado becomes a stage for cultural and gastronomic gatherings, while trendy parties and open-air celebrations keep the region lively well into the first days of January.

For those who prefer a slower pace, destinations such as Morro de São Paulo, Itacaré and Boipeba offer the perfect combination of paradise beaches, hiking trails, boat tours and intimate parties. In these areas, it is possible to alternate between days immersed in nature and lively nights with live music and beachfront celebrations.

Beyond the parties, the end of the year in Bahia is ideal for cultural tours, visits to historic centers, handicraft fairs, gastronomic experiences and activities connected to Afro-Brazilian culture. Whether for those seeking major events, exclusive parties or moments of rest surrounded by nature, Bahia offers options for every style, establishing itself as one of the most complete and vibrant destinations to celebrate the arrival of a new year.

item.title: New Year’s Eve in Ceará: Where Summer Meets Celebration
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Ceará stands out as one of the most sought-after destinations in Brazil’s Northeast during the end-of-year festivities. With warm weather, impressive natural landscapes and a program that combines major events, leisure and close contact with nature, the state offers experiences for different traveler profiles as the year comes to an end.

In Fortaleza, the main attraction is the Fortaleza New Year’s Eve Celebration, held along the Praia de Iracema waterfront and considered one of the largest in the country. The event features free concerts by national artists, a fireworks display and infrastructure designed to welcome thousands of people. In addition to public celebrations, the capital offers private parties, special hotel programs, bars and beach clubs, as well as a wide range of dining options. During the day, urban beaches such as Meireles, Mucuripe and Praia do Futuro are ideal for enjoying the sun, practicing sports and soaking up the city’s relaxed atmosphere.

For those seeking quieter and more exclusive settings, Ceará’s coastline offers destinations such as Jericoacoara, which becomes even more charming at the end of the year. The village features intimate parties, beachfront celebrations, buggy rides across the dunes, crystal-clear lagoons and sunsets that turn into true natural spectacles during this season.

Other destinations that gain prominence include Canoa Quebrada, Icaraí de Amontada and Flecheiras, known for combining natural beauty, water sports, quality gastronomy and lively festivities that energize the nights of December and January. These regions attract both visitors looking to relax and those who want to celebrate New Year’s Eve in a laid-back yet sophisticated atmosphere.

Beyond the parties, the end of the year in Ceará is ideal for outdoor experiences, such as jangada boat rides, hiking trails, kitesurfing, visits to cliffs, lagoons and coastal villages. Handicraft fairs, regional markets and cultural events are also part of the program, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in the local identity.

With options ranging from large urban celebrations to seaside natural retreats, Ceará establishes itself as a complete destination for celebrating the end of the year, offering sunshine, joy and unforgettable experiences to welcome the new cycle.

item.title: From Waterfalls to Fireworks: End-of-Year Experiences in Foz do Iguaçu
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Foz do Iguaçu stands out as one of Brazil’s most complete destinations for end-of-year festivities, combining breathtaking nature, cultural attractions and a special program to celebrate the arrival of a new cycle. During this period, the city takes on a festive atmosphere and welcomes visitors seeking unique experiences set against one of the most impressive natural landscapes in the world.

One of the main highlights of the season is a visit to the Iguazu Falls, which become even more spectacular in summer, when water levels are usually higher. Walking along the trails, admiring the waterfalls and enjoying activities within the Iguazu National Park is an unmissable experience for those spending the end of the year in the city.

In the urban area, special Christmas and New Year’s Eve programs take center stage, featuring cultural performances, themed decorations, live shows and fireworks at key locations around the city. Hotels and resorts offer festive dinners, New Year’s Eve parties and exclusive activities for guests, often with privileged views and full-service experiences.

Another highlight is the Triple Frontier between Brazil, Argentina and Paraguay, allowing visitors to experience different cultures within just a few days. Travelers can cross the border to enjoy Argentine cuisine in Puerto Iguazú or go shopping in Ciudad del Este, adding even more diversity to the holiday experience.

For those interested in culture and history, the Itaipu Hydroelectric Power Plant offers special tours and nighttime illumination shows that captivate both locals and tourists, especially during the holiday season. The Three Borders Landmark is also one of the most popular spots to enjoy the sunset and take part in end-of-year celebrations, with cultural performances and a well-structured gastronomic area.

Beyond the festivities, Foz do Iguaçu is ideal for travelers looking to combine leisure, relaxation and contact with nature. Trails, boat tours, theme parks and natural reserves round out the experience, offering options for different travel styles. Whether celebrating New Year’s Eve in a festive atmosphere or welcoming the new year surrounded by nature, Foz do Iguaçu proves to be a versatile and unforgettable destination for end-of-year celebrations.
item.category: New Year’s Eve in Foz do Iguaçu

item.title: Luxury Experiences in Brazil: A High-Value Destination for North American Travelers
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
As North American travelers begin planning their luxury trips for the year ahead, Brazil continues to strengthen its position as a high-value destination for travel advisors seeking differentiated, experience-driven products. The country combines exclusivity and premium services with nature-focused journeys, sophisticated coastal escapes and must-see attractions recognized worldwide.

Luxury tourism in Brazil has grown consistently, attracting travelers who value tailor-made itineraries, meaningful experiences and high-end services set within some of the most diverse natural and cultural landscapes in the world. From preserved coastlines to wildlife-rich ecosystems and globally recognized natural wonders, Brazil delivers a luxury proposition rooted in authenticity, sustainability and immersive travel.

Below are luxury experiences that highlight how Brazil, particularly the states of Ceará, Bahia, Mato Grosso do Sul and Iguassu Falls, can be positioned as highly attractive and exclusive destinations for North American clients.

Ceará – Coastal Luxury, Privacy and Sophisticated Leisure

Ceará stands out for its combination of dramatic landscapes, refined beachfront hospitality and exclusive outdoor experiences. Destinations such as Jericoacoara offer a fusion of understated luxury and natural beauty, featuring boutique accommodations, private beach access and breathtaking sunsets over sand dunes and lagoons.

Along the coast, high-end resorts in regions such as Cumbuco and Aquiraz provide a complete luxury experience, with spas, golf courses, sophisticated gastronomy and personalized services, all set against privileged oceanfront views.

Luxury experiences in Ceará typically include private buggy or quad bike tours across dunes and secluded beaches, VIP beach club structures and curated gastronomic experiences in Fortaleza and surrounding areas, such as private tastings, chef-led dinners and cultural encounters focused on local flavors and traditions.

In addition, Ceará expands its luxury tourism offering with scenic helicopter flights along the coastline, revealing panoramic views of dunes, cliffs and crystal-clear lagoons. The state is also recognized for premium sports experiences, including private kitesurfing and windsurfing lessons in ideal natural conditions, as well as beachfront wellness retreats that combine holistic therapies, healthy cuisine and a strong sense of exclusivity and seclusion.

Bahia – Cultural Sophistication and Barefoot Luxury

Bahia presents one of Brazil’s most distinctive luxury propositions, blending history, Afro-Brazilian culture and exclusive coastal retreats. Regions such as Trancoso, Itacaré and the Cocoa Coast are known for the concept of barefoot luxury, featuring boutique hotels and high-end pousadas that offer privacy, oceanfront settings and personalized services in harmony with nature.

Luxury travelers can enjoy private transfers, customized coastal itineraries, moonlit beach dinners and tailor-made experiences focused on relaxation, gastronomy and local culture.

In Salvador, premium experiences include private guided visits to historic neighborhoods and UNESCO-listed heritage sites, cultural immersions centered on Afro-Brazilian traditions, and exclusive workshops with local artists, musicians and cultural experts, all designed for travelers seeking depth, context and authenticity.

Bahia’s luxury appeal is further enhanced by exclusive nautical experiences, such as private yacht or sailing trips through the Bay of All Saints, with stops at preserved islands and onboard gourmet service. Additional offerings include private cooking classes featuring Bahian cuisine, nature-integrated wellness retreats and privileged access to major cultural events and traditional festivities, delivering a refined immersion into the destination’s identity.

Mato Grosso do Sul – High-End Wildlife Encounters in the Pantanal

Mato Grosso do Sul offers one of South America’s most remarkable luxury nature experiences through the Pantanal, the world’s largest tropical wetland. In this region, luxury lodges combine comfort, privacy and expert guiding to deliver exceptional wildlife encounters.

Experiences include guided safaris focused on jaguar and bird observation, river excursions at sunrise and sunset, and immersive stays that balance adventure with refined service. High-end lodges typically offer full-board accommodations, bilingual naturalist guides, private transfers and fully customized itineraries.

Additional premium options include photographic safaris, private boat and kayak excursions, exclusive nature picnics and starlit dinners, ideal for travelers seeking a transformative luxury experience guided by nature and rooted in strong conservation values.

Complementing land and river activities, the Pantanal also offers private panoramic flights showcasing the vastness of the floodplain, as well as high-end sustainable sport fishing programs. Many lodges provide educational conservation experiences, allowing luxury travelers to engage directly with researchers and preservation initiatives while enjoying purposeful and immersive travel.

Iguassu Falls – Iconic Nature with Five-Star Service

Iguassu Falls is home to one of the world’s most iconic natural landmarks. Luxury tourism in the region is defined by impeccable service, privileged access and deeply immersive nature-based experiences.

Five-star properties located within or near the national park offer early or exclusive access to the falls, refined dining experiences and personalized excursions. Luxury activities include private helicopter flights over the waterfalls, expert-led ecological walks and photography-focused tours highlighting the region’s rich biodiversity.

Additional curated experiences combine visits to cultural and environmental attractions with high-end gastronomy and private transportation, creating balanced itineraries that seamlessly blend nature, comfort and cultural discovery.

Luxury travel in Iguassu Falls is further elevated through customized cross-border experiences connecting Brazil, Argentina and Paraguay. Highlights include VIP visits to the Itaipu Hydroelectric Plant, private dinners in strategically located venues with privileged views, and carefully curated evening experiences that merge fine dining, regional culture and exclusive access to emblematic attractions.

Positioning Brazil for Success in the Luxury Travel Market

For North American travel advisors and tour operators, Brazil’s luxury offerings represent valuable opportunities to create highly customized, high-yield itineraries across multiple segments, including honeymoons, multigenerational travel, special-interest tourism and soft-adventure experiences.

With efficient domestic air connectivity, private transfers and well-established local concierge services, Brazil enables the design of complex itineraries that combine coastline, wildlife, culture, wellness and iconic attractions, consistently delivering memorable, market-ready experiences aligned with the expectations of today’s luxury traveler.

Through initiatives such as Brasil DNA, tourism professionals gain access to curated insights, trusted partners and destination intelligence, reinforcing Brazil’s positioning as a premium, experience-driven destination in the North American market.

item.title: Pantanal Jam brings the Pantanal to the international stage uniting music, conservation and social impact
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
The sound of the Pantanal gained international prominence with the arrival of Pantanal Jam, a project that connects contemporary jazz, the sounds of nature, and environmental conservation.

Featuring performances by the award-winning Brazilian group Urbem and the participation of renowned American trombonist Ryan Keberle, Pantanal Jam offers an immersive experience that translates the essence of the Pantanal biome through music. The nine original compositions incorporate sound recordings of wildlife, landscapes, and the waters of the Pantanal, with recordings made within the territory itself, expanding the dialogue between art, nature, and nature conservation.

More than a musical project, Pantanal Jam is consolidated as a platform for raising awareness of the Pantanal biome by giving visibility to the work of NGOs that operate in the protection of biodiversity and the strengthening of local communities. The event stage also becomes a space for exchange, with discussion circles, workshops, and exhibitions that present stories, challenges, and achievements of the institutions involved.

Among the organizations highlighted are Instituto SOS Pantanal, Instituto Homem Pantaneiro (IHP), Instituto Arara Azul, and Onçafari, national references in conservation actions, environmental education, wildfire prevention and combat, and the protection of endangered species. Part of the revenue generated by the project will be allocated to supporting these initiatives, contributing to actions such as community fire brigades, ecological restoration, wildlife rehabilitation, and environmental education programs.

“Pantanal Jam presents itself as a bridge between art, nature, and conservation, capable of touching people and strengthening the identity of the Pantanal and its communities,” emphasizes the Tourism Foundation of Mato Grosso do Sul.

By bringing the Pantanal to the center of one of the world’s leading cultural hubs, the project reinforces the potential of culture as a tool for global awareness and positions the biome as a symbol of biodiversity, sustainability, and nature tourism on the global stage.

Acess Pantanal Jam for more: https://pantanaljam.com/ 

  • Documentary: https://pantanaljam.com/#film 
  • Album: https://pantanaljam.com/#album 

The real Brazil is waiting-colorful, festive, and full of unforgettable stories.

item.title: Experiential travel shapes Valentine’s Day getaways as traveleres seek meaning over gifts
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
As Valentine’s Day approaches, global travel trends indicate that travelers are increasingly shifting from traditional urban celebrations toward immersive, nature-based experiences. Rather than city-centered itineraries, many of them are prioritizing destinations that offer meaningful connection, outdoor exploration, and opportunities to disconnect from routine.

According to the Experiential Travel Trends 2026 report by Accor and Globetrender, travelers are placing growing value on emotionally engaging and immersive experiences, with an emphasis on social wellness, nature, and authentic interaction over conventional sightseeing. The report identifies a structural shift in travel behavior toward trips that foster deeper personal connection and shared discovery.

American Express Travel’s global trend reporting similarly notes that a majority of travelers are planning trips centered on memory-making and purpose-driven experiences rather than purely transactional tourism. Couples are increasingly prioritizing destinations that combine relaxation, adventure, and environmental immersion.

Within this broader industry context, Brazil’s Mato Grosso do Sul has recorded measurable tourism growth alongside its sustainability-driven positioning. Official state data show a 5.6% increase in passenger arrivals at the state’s main airports between January and October 2025, totaling 724,662 arrivals, compared to 685,700 during the same period in 2024. In Bonito and the Serra da Bodoquena region, key ecotourism hubs, 727,819 visitor vouchers were issued, while Bonito alone recorded 247,049 tourists, representing a 3% increase year over year.

Home to a significant portion of the Pantanal, widely recognized as the world’s largest tropical wetland, Mato Grosso do Sul offers structured wildlife observation, including regulated jaguar and birdwatching experiences. In Bonito, tourism operates under a controlled voucher system that limits daily visitor numbers and manages access to natural attractions, a governance model frequently cited in Brazil as a best-practice approach to ecotourism management.

These mechanisms align with growing global demand for responsible and low-impact travel. Industry analysts describe experiential travel as one in which outdoor activities, wellness-oriented itineraries, and environmental storytelling are central to trip planning. Increasingly, couples are seeking destinations where shared activities, such as snorkeling in protected freshwater springs or observing wildlife in preserved ecosystems, provide opportunities for meaningful connection beyond traditional celebratory formats.

As Valentine’s Day travel continues to evolve, Mato Grosso do Sul represents a case study in how biodiversity-rich regions can align conservation policy, visitor management, and international promotion with changing traveler behavior.

item.title: Music, conservation, and social impact: Pantanal Jam and the spotlight on the work of NGOs
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
Far beyond uniting jazz and the natural musicality of the Pantanal, the Pantanal Jam project stood out for bringing focus to the conservation of the Pantanal territory, native species, and regional culture. Reinforcing its main purpose: giving visibility to the work of NGOs that work daily to protect the Pantanal biome, its fauna, flora, and the communities of the region.

Transforming its stage into a showcase for projects that preserve endangered species, promote environmental education, and develop social impact actions with riverside communities, Indigenous peoples, and small producers.

NGOs that make a difference in the Pantanal.

Among the organizations present are institutions nationally recognized for their continuous work in defense of the biome, such as:

Instituto SOS Pantanal - A nonprofit organization that works in the prevention and combat of forest fires through the Pantaneira Brigades, restoration of degraded areas, protection of springs, defense of the Pantanal biome, and the dissemination of its importance. The institute uses science and projects to defend the biome, seeking to protect biodiversity and Pantanal culture.

Instituto Homem Pantaneiro (IHP) - A civil society, nonprofit organization founded in 2002 that works to preserve the Pantanal biome and local culture. Its activities include the management of protected areas, promotion of research, environmental monitoring, recovery of degraded areas, conservation of biodiversity corridors, and the development of programs such as Rede Amolar and the Memorial Homem Pantaneiro.

Instituto Arara Azul - Created by biologist Neiva Guedes, the institute has been working since 1990 with management, monitoring, and environmental education projects to ensure the survival of the species of "arara-azul" (blue macaw) and the preservation of its habitat.

Onçafari - It is dedicated to biodiversity conservation and the preservation of wild animals, with a special focus on onça-pintada. Onçafari seeks to promote coexistence between wildlife and local communities, using scientific research and ecotourism as its main tools.

In August 2025, Onçafari and the Instituto Homem Pantaneiro announced a partnership to expand fire prevention actions in the Pantanal, with joint work of the environmental brigade across more than 285 thousand hectares.

"For Onçafari, conservation is not done alone. The partnership with IHP, in the anti-fire front, is a clear example of how collaboration between institutions can make a difference. Together, we not only protect the rich biodiversity of the Pantanal but also strengthen fire prevention actions." Onçafari's statement.

At Pantanal Jam, in addition to the musicality, the public will be able to follow discussion circles, workshops, and exhibitions where NGO members will share stories, challenges, and achievements. The project is not just entertainment; it is also a space to give voice to those who fight daily for the survival of the Pantanal.

"Pantanal Jam presents itself explicitly as a bridge between art, nature, and conservation," emphasizes the Tourism Foundation of Mato Grosso do Sul.

Part of the revenue will be allocated to support the projects of the participating NGOs, contributing to the funding of fire-combat brigades, wildlife rehabilitation, ecological restoration, and environmental education. The initiative also aims to give visibility to sustainable practices and promote nature tourism, with direct benefits to local communities.

Acess Pantanal Jam for more: https://pantanaljam.com/ 

  • Documentary: https://pantanaljam.com/#film 
  • Album: https://pantanaljam.com/#album 

Come discover the beauty beyond nature.
The real Brazil is waiting-colorful, festive, and full of unforgettable stories.

item.title: Pantanal Jam: When Nature Becomes the Composer
item.dc:creator: BrasilDNA
item.content:encoded:
The Pantanal, the world's largest tropical wetland, is not just a backdrop for Pantanal Jam. It is the project's co-composer. Born from a shared desire to create music that could honor, listen to, and protect one of the most threatened biomes on the planet, the project began after members of Urbem and Ryan Keberle first visited the region and were struck by its sonic richness and environmental vulnerability.

Pantanal Jam is the result of an unprecedented artistic collaboration between Brazilian ensemble Urbem and New York-based trombonist Ryan Keberle. Rather than adding nature sounds in post-production, the artists spent over a year conducting field research and recording inside the biome. The album, titled Pantanal Jam, and its accompanying documentary short film, Pantanal Jam: A Nature-Made Session, capture this process in its purest form, a true dialogue between jazz improvisation and a living ecosystem.

"The Pantanal doesn't just inspire the music it directs it," says Urbem drummer Bruno Duarte. "There were moments when a single bird call changed the entire structure of a piece. We had to relearn how to listen."

For Keberle, the experience reshaped his relationship with improvisation: "Playing with the landscape taught us to let go of control. The environment had its own tempo, and we followed."

In this project, the Pantanal becomes a living studio

Its unpredictability - the sudden call of a thrush, the low growl of a jaguar, the shift in light over the flooded plains - shaped the tempo, texture, and emotional core of the compositions. Each track is built on this exchange: humans listening deeply to the environment, and the environment responding in its own voice.

But Pantanal Jam is not only about music

It is also a soundtrack for conservation. Listeners are invited not only to explore the album, but also to support the region directly. Donations made through the official website go entirely to organizations working in the Pantanal, funding wildlife monitoring, habitat protection, and environmental education. A portion of proceeds from the album also contributes to these initiatives turning each stream, share, or contribution into tangible impact.

At its heart, Pantanal Jam is also a story of cultural collaboration. The meeting between Urbem's Brazilian roots and Keberle's New York jazz sensibility embodies the project's guiding idea: creativity thrives in connection. Two musical cultures and two artistic perspectives unite under a shared purpose to honor and help protect an extraordinary ecosystem.

Pantanal Jam invites listeners to experience the Pantanal not through images, but through sound - to hear a place that few people in the world will ever visit, yet everyone can help protect. Because in the end, to listen is already to care.

Acess Pantanal Jam for more: https://pantanaljam.com/ 

  • Documentary: https://pantanaljam.com/#film 
  • Album: https://pantanaljam.com/#album 

The real Brazil is waiting-colorful, festive, and full of unforgettable stories.

item.title: Mato Grosso do Sul to Present Climate-Resilient Tourism Model at the One Planet Global Roundtable
item.dc:creator: Henrique
item.content:encoded:
From Bonito to the world: sustainability and environmental governance at the center of the global debate.

Mato Grosso do Sul will participate in the One Planet Global Roundtable – Practical Solutions for a Climate Resilient Tourism Future, an initiative promoted by the Green Initiative that brings together public leaders, experts, and representatives of destinations committed to concrete solutions for adapting to and mitigating the impacts of climate change in tourism.

The meeting is part of the international agenda of the One Planet movement, focused on building a more resilient tourism sector, with an emphasis on decarbonization, environmental governance, and sustainable development. The proposal of the Roundtable is to present practical experiences and replicable models that contribute to the structural evolution of the global travel industry.

The state will be represented by Bruno Wendling, President of the Mato Grosso do Sul Tourism Foundation, who will present the model implemented in Bonito — a destination internationally recognized for its structured environmental governance.

Since 2023, Bonito has accumulated international awards and recognitions related to sustainability and responsible tourism management. The destination consolidated its position as a benchmark after becoming the world’s first carbon-neutral ecotourism destination, in addition to receiving certifications and distinctions in awards focused on climate action and best practices in public sector management within the tourism sector.

The adopted model includes carbon emissions inventorying and offsetting, strict control of tourism carrying capacity, continuous environmental monitoring, and integration between the public sector, the tourism trade, and the local community — pillars that strengthen the destination’s competitiveness in an international landscape increasingly guided by ESG criteria.

For the tourism trade, participation in the Roundtable represents more than institutional visibility: it is a strategic window for Brazilian tourism in the global debate on climate and sustainability.

“We believe Brazil has full capacity to take a leading role in the global resilient tourism agenda. What we will present at the Roundtable is not an isolated project, but a structured policy with goals, indicators, and concrete results. Our objective is to demonstrate that it is possible to grow with environmental responsibility and that Brazilian tourism can lead this movement,” says Bruno Wendling.

The presence at the event is also part of the destination’s international promotion strategy, carried out in partnership with Global Vision Access in the United States, strengthening its positioning in priority markets and among strategic stakeholders.

By sharing its trajectory at a global forum dedicated to building applicable climate solutions, Mato Grosso do Sul reaffirms its vision for the future: to develop tourism as a driver of prosperity, environmental conservation, and innovation — contributing to positioning Brazil as a benchmark in sustainable tourism on the international stage.

For more information and event registration, visit One Planet Global Roundtable.

Learn more about Mato Grosso do Sul’s content in the U.S. at: GVA News.
